retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years

series_title:
Statewide and Regional
Poison Control Program
Plans
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years

series_description:
Including any supporting
documentation created and/or used to
justify and support legislative
appropriations requests by an agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Legislative Appropriation
Requests must be submitted to
the Texas State Publications
Depository Program per
13TAC3.3(a)(3)(A). Working files
and related documentation used
in creating the final plan are not
subject to archival review and may
be disposed of at the expiration of
the retention period.

series_description:
Any document that officially initiates,
rescinds, or amends general office
procedures.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description:
Any document that initiates, rescinds,
or amends a regulation, policy, or
procedure that governs the programs,
services, or projects of an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_title:
Calendars,
Appointment, and
Itinerary Information
Records
series_description:
Calendars, appointment books or
programs and scheduling, or itinerary
records maintained by staff during
business hours that document
appointments, itineraries, and other
activities of agency officials or
employees.
retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 1 year
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTES: Only the
calendars, appointment, and
itinerary records of elected
officials, agency heads, and board
or commission members require
archival review. Contact the State
Archives when these records have
met their retention periods.
CAUTION: A record of this type
purchased with personal funds but
used by a state official or
employee to document his or her
work activities may be a state
record and subject to this
retention period. See Open
Records Decision 635 issued in
December 1995 by he Attorney
General.

series_description:
News, press releases, marketing files
and collateral, or any public relations
files maintained or issued by an
agency. Includes print, electronic,
audio, and audiovisual records.

Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are furnished to the public
under the Public Information Act
(Chapter 552, Government Code). Also
includes withdrawn requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are excepted under the
Public Information Act (Chapter 552,
Government Code), including records
provided to the legislature under
section 552.008.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Charts or diagrams that show the
structure of an agency and the
relationships and relative ranks of its
departments and job positions.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTES: Only charts
showing the overall arrangement
and administrative structure of
the agency need to be transferred
to the Archives and Information
Services Division, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.
Organizational charts showing
division/department level detail
are not considered archival.

series_description:
Plans and records relating to the
process of planning new or redefined
programs, services, or projects of an
agency that are neither included in nor
directly related to other records series
in this schedule.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Data processing
planning records are not archival.

series_description:
Copies of all proposed, withdrawn,
emergency, and adopted rules; open
meetings notices; or any other
documents required by law to be
submitted to the Texas Register.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Drafts of proposed legislation and
related correspondence.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Notes or text of speeches, papers,
presentations, or reports delivered in
conjunction with agency work.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only speeches,
papers, and presentations given
by or on behalf of executive staff,
board or commission members,
division directors and program
heads require archival review.

series_description:
Records created by or on behalf of an
agency in anticipation of or in the
adjudication of a lawsuit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Cases that set
legal precedent or exhibit
historical value will be evaluated
by the Archives and Information
Services Division of the Texas
State Library and Archives
Commission for archival
preservation.

series_description:
Logs or similar records used to register
persons appearing before state
agencies as required by Chapter 2004,
Government Code, including quarterly
reports filed with the Texas Ethics
Commission (TEC).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Operational strategic plans, which
include descriptions of information
resources programs, prepared in
accordance with Government Code,
2054.095 and 2056.002. Includes
working files and related
documentation used in creating the
final plan.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Strategic Plans must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(C).
Working files and related
documentation used in creating
the final plan are not subject to
archival review and may be
disposed of at the expiration of
the retention period.

series_description:
Self-evaluations and plans
documenting compliance with the
requirements of the Americans with
Disabilities Act.

Official agenda and minutes of state
boards, committees, commissions, and
councils that conduct open meetings as
required by Government Code, Chapter
551.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Agency retains
permanent record copy. The
archival requirement will be met
by sending a copy to the Archives
and Information Services Division,
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission. As a general practice
the Commission does not create
minutes of open meetings.
CAUTION: This records series and
item numbers 1.1.059, 1.1.060,
1.1.061, and 1.1.062 must be used
for those state boards,
committees, commissions, and
councils, which by law or the
biennial Appropriations Act, are
administered by another state
agency. These records and all
others related to the functions of
any of these dependent entities
must be included in the records
retention schedule of the
administering agency.

series_description:
Minutes or notes, and supporting
documentation, taken at internal
agency staff meetings.

Biennial narrative reports to the
governor and legislature as required by
an agency's enabling statutes, including
annual narrative reports if they are
required by statute. Includes working
files and related documentation used
in creating final report.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of these agency reports must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13TAC33. The requirement is
met by sending the required
copies of the reports to the Texas
State Publications Depository
Program, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission. Working
files and related documentation
used in creating the final report
may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.

series_title:
Agency Rules, Policies,
and Procedures-
Working Files & Final
series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, administrative
rules, or similar records distributed
internally for the use of employees or
externally to the public or those
individuals or entities regulated by an
agency that sets out the rules, policies,
and procedures that govern an
agency's programs, services, or
projects. Includes working files and
related documentation used in creating
rules, policies, and procedures.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Working files
and related documentation used
in creating the final rules, policies,
and procedures, are not subject to
archival requirement and may be
disposed of at the expiration of
the retention period.

series_description:
Reports made to the Office of Attorney
General (OAG) on an agency's Public
Information Act activities, per
Government Code, 552.010.

Transcripts and final decisions of
hearings conducted as part of the
regulatory process, and hearings on
proposed rules and changes.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only records
that lead to substantial or
statutory changes to the
operations of the agency and its
policies and procedures should be
retained for archival review.
Contact the State Archives when
these records have met their
retention periods.
CAUTION: These records may be
maintained with related
information, including meeting
notices, proofs of publication, and
meeting minutes, and require a
longer retention period.

series_title:
Sunset Review Report
and Related
Documentation
series_description:
Sunset Review Report, agency self-
study, and other correspondence and
supporting documentation related to
the Sunset review process for state
agencies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Related
documentation includes the
agency self-evaluation report,
agency response to the
commission's draft report, and
documentation of the agency's
implementation of the
commission's final
recommendations.

series_description:
Final agreement described by
Government Code, 2009.054(c),
associated with a matter conducted
under an alternative dispute resolution
procedure in which personnel of a
state agency participated as a party on
the agency's behalf.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
legal_citation:
Texas Civil Practice and Remedies
Code, Chapter 154.071.

series_description:
Master versions of all forms used
internally and externally by the agency,
including subsequent revisions to an
agency form or any associated design
or design modification requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
A records retention schedule (i.e. form
SLR 105) that identifies the records
that are created and maintained by an
agency, provides the minimum
timeframes the records must be
retained, and includes instructions for
their disposition. This series may
include working files and
documentation of certification and
approval by the Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
Original is retained for 50 years by
the State and Local Records
Management Division, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.

series_description:
Forms used to track the transmittal of
records to/from onsite and offsite
storage areas or a transfer of physical
custody.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Logs or similar records listing records
disposed of by an agency, which might
include records series title, dates of
records, and date of disposition.
comments:
CAUTION: Disposition can mean
destroyed or transferred.

series_description:
Records management plans and similar
records that establish the policies and
procedures under which records and
information are managed in an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
To assure Records Management is
maintaining the plan.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description:
Also includes documentation for
disaster recovery services provided by
other entities.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 1 year

series_description: Agency copy form.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Relatively long-lived computer files
containing organized and consistent
sets of complete and accurate
electronic records and associated
processing files. Examples include, but
are not limited to, data tables of
relational databases used by
applications or computer programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Records management
officers must be certain before
assigning the retention period of
AC to agency master files and
application data that the files do
not fall under records series listed
elsewhere.

series_description:
Automated software applications and
operating system files including job
control language, program
listing/source code, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Records documenting operational and
maintenance requirements of
computer hardware such as operating
manuals, hardware/operating system
requirements, hardware
configurations, equipment control
systems, and associated processing
files.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Hardware needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Records adequate to specify all
technical characteristics necessary for
reading or processing electronic
records; to document modifications to
computer programs; to access,
retrieve, manipulate, and interpret
data in an automated system; and
allow for their timely, authorized
disposition. Records include but are
not limited to: user guides, system or
sub-system definitions, system
specifications, input and output
specifications, system flow charts,
program flow charts, program
maintenance logs, change notices, data
element dictionaries, file layouts, code
books or tables, and other records that
explain the meaning, purpose,
structure, logical relationships, and
origin of the data elements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Hardware and software
needed for access to electronic
records must be retained for the
period of time required to access
the records.

series_description:
Information verifying the quality of
system, hardware, or software
operations. Records include but are not
limited to: records of errors or failures
and the loss of data resulting from such
failures; documentation of abnormal
termination and of error- free
processing; checks of changes put into
production; transaction histories; and
other records needed as an audit trail
to evaluate data accuracy.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_title:
Software Registrations,
Warranties, and License
Agreements
series_description:
Records documenting the registration
and licensing of a software application
to activate the software for legal use
by the end users of a state agency,
along with warranties providing that
the software will perform in
accordance with functional
specifications.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years

series_description:
Applications, resumes, transcripts,
letters of reference, and similar
documents whose submission by
candidates for vacant positions is
required on the application form, by
application procedures, or in the
employment advertisement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Notes, memoranda, or reports relating
to the counseling of an employee for
work-related, personal, or substance
abuse problems. Usually maintained at
the supervisorial level except in those
agencies with counseling staff.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Internal or external announcements or
advertisements of job openings,
promotions, training programs, or
opportunities for overtime.

Includes a contract or agreement
regarding the terms and conditions of
employment of an individual and a
contract with a vendor for temporary
staffing services.
Executed, renewed, or amended on or
after September 1, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Includes a contract or agreement
regarding the terms and conditions of
employment of an individual and a
contract with a vendor for temporary
staffing services.
Executed, renewed, or amended on or
before August 31, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
comments:
NOTE: Refer to SB20 (84th Leg.)
for retention period context.

series_description:
Job evaluations, performance
appraisals, or other similar documents
used to evaluate the performance of
an employee.

Disciplinary actions are those actions
which may affect pay, status, or
tenure. They are imposed to discipline
an employee whose conduct is harmful
to the best interests of the state, the
agency, or the employee work force;
and for failure to improve performance
or conduct following imposition of
corrective action. May include cause
for demotion, suspension, or dismissal,
and the reasons for failure to give
written notice of resignation. May also
be used to document evidence of
employee self- improvement efforts, as
well as favorable and unfavorable
communication.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Forms or similar records used to create
or change information concerning the
records of an employee, including pay
grade, position classification, employee
number, evaluation date, and
termination of employment.

Job descriptions, including all
associated task or skill statements, for
positions in an agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Criminal history record information on
job applicants or agency employees
obtained from the Department of
Public Safety (DPS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Certificates of completion,
certifications, licenses, transcripts, test
scores, or similar records documenting
the training, testing, certification,
licensing, or continuing education
achievements of an employee.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_title:
Employment Eligibility
Documentation or
Verification of
series_description: Federal reporting form (Form I-9).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
CAUTION: Federal regulation
requires that INS 1-9 forms be
retained for 3 years from date of
hire or 1 year after separation of
the employee, whichever later.
Agencies should make certain that
the INS I-9 forms for employees
who terminate from an agency
less than 3 year retention period.

series_description: Awards, incentives, tenure, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Documentation used to start, modify,
or stop all voluntary or required
deductions from payroll, including
garnishment or other court-ordered
attachments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Payroll records and registers
documenting employee earnings,
wages, and pay. This may include but is
not limited to payroll input records,
summary statements, payroll vouchers,
payroll detail sheets, and payroll
history.

Includes 1099, W2, FICA, and other tax
records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Used to make increases or decreases to
employees' gross pay, FICA,
retirement, or in the computation of
taxes.

Employer's copy of "Employee’s
Withholding Exemption Certificate."
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Records and documentation relating to
unemployment compensation claims,
including reimbursement of funds
disbursed by Texas Workforce
Commission (TWC).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
Open unless confidential
personnel information included in
report.

series_description:
Forms used to deposit employee’s
earnings into a specified personal
account.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Records documenting the amount of
pension or deferred compensation
earned by individual employees.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
comments:
Retirement or termination of the
employee. Vendors for the
individual participant have been
closed. For instructions regarding
the determination of the closure
of accounts and for additional
information regarding the
retention period see the most
current edition of the Benefits
Coordinator Reference Manual
issued by the Employees
Retirement System of Texas.

series_description:
Affirmative action plans for regular
employees and apprenticeship
programs.

Employee benefit plans such as
pension; life, health, and disability
insurance; deferred compensation;
etc., including amendments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Reports providing statistical
information on labor force.

Minimum information needed to verify
employment. Includes: name; social
security number; exact dates of
employment; last known address; and
most recent public access
authorization.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 75 years

series_description:
Records relating to review and
monitoring of job classifications within
an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Reports to the agency from TWC or its
predecessor pertaining to employees.

series_title:
Reimbursable Activity
Requests and
Authorizations to
Engage in
series_description:
Requests and authorizations for travel;
participation in educational programs,
workshops, or college classes; or for
other work-related activities for which
the expenses of the employee are
defrayed or reimbursed.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Any internally distributed manuals,
guidelines, or similar records that
define agency wide policies and
procedures concerning the personnel
of an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Any documents detailing the
procedural duties and responsibilities
of agency positions on a position-by-
position basis.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Any reports compiled by an agency on
aspects of personnel staffing, including
listings of all staff by program or name,
staff hired during a month, detailed
listings of employees within its
organizational structure, position
vacancies, analyses of turnover rates
and seasonality of employment, etc.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Instructional materials and other
records associated with in-house
training of agency on personnel policies
and procedures and other policies and
procedures that govern an agency's
programs, services, or projects.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 2 years

series_description:
Includes documentation used to
complete EEO (Equal Employment
Opportunity) reports.

Reports, studies, aggregated or
summary data, and similar
documentation compiled to monitor
and demonstrate compliance with the
federal Equal Pay Act.

Used to create employee leave
balances, to transfer leave balances
when an employee transfers positions,
to adjust carry-over balances at end of
fiscal year, to correct errors on leave
accumulation, and to close out leave
accounts on separated employees.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Cumulative report is issued each pay
cycle and provides employee leave
status information for each position.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Records documenting individual
employee’s hours worked, including
work schedules and documentation
evidencing adherence to or deviation
from normal hours for those
employees working on fixed schedules.

series_title:
Time Off and/or Sick
Leave Requests
series_description:
Requests and authorizations for
vacation, compensatory, sick, Family
and Medical Leave Act (FMLA), sick
pool leave, and other types of
authorized leave, and supporting
documentation.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Records documenting number of hours
transferred in and out of
comprehensive sick leave pool.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Claims; invoices; statements; copies of
checks and purchase orders;
expenditure authorizations; ledgers;
encumbrance, purchase, general
journal, expenditure, and special
vouchers; and similar records that
serve to document disbursements,
including those documenting claims for
and reimbursement to employees for
travel and other expenses. Includes DIR
TEXAN.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_title:
9-1-1 Service Fee and
Equalization Surcharge
Revenues/Collections
series_description:
Includes Wireline, Wireless, VoIP, and
Equalization Surcharge revenue and
collections documentation.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 15 years

series_description: Supporting Documentation
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
Public Utility Commission retains
original applications and
supporting documentation.

series_description:
Periodic reports compiled from
information entered into the Uniform
Statewide Accounting System (USAS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
AC= Receipt and reconciliation of
annual report.

series_description:
Yearly report compiled from
information entered into the Uniform
Statewide Accounting System (USAS).
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Records documenting the internal and
external procedural requirements with
respect to the accounting department
of a state agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Documentation relating to the capital
and fixed assets of a state agency,
including equipment or property
history cards containing data on initial
cost, depreciation schedules or
summaries used for capital outlay
budgeting, and property sale, auction,
or disposal records of agency owned
equipment and property.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years

series_description:
This series documents state, federal,
and other sponsored grant projects
participated in or administered by state
agencies. It includes grant
authorization records, which provide
evidence of the award of grants to or
by agencies; grant/project financial or
performance reports, which are
periodic reports of financial activity
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: Retention
requirements may vary depending
on the specific federal funding
agency. Records in this series may
be subject to Government Code
441.1855. Agencies must ensure
that records are retained for the
appropriate retention period.

series_description:
Signatu re cards o r similar reco rds
establishing authority of an agency
employee to initiate or authorize
financial transactions on behalf of an
agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Any internally distributed manual,
guidelines, or similar records that
establish standard office procedures
for an agency, e.g. agency style
manuals, telephone protocols, mail
room procedures, print shop and
photocopy ordering instructions.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description:
Any record created by an agency to
track any type of incoming and
outgoing correspondence or packages
by the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) or by
private couriers.

Includes bid requisition/authorizations,
invitations to bid or propose, bid
specifications, awarded and
unawarded bids, and bid tabulation
/evaluations.
Associated with a contract executed,
renewed, or amended on or after
September 1, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Includes bid requisition/authorizations,
invitations to bid or propose, bid
specifications, awarded and
unawarded bids, and bid tabulation
/evaluations.
Associated with a contract executed,
renewed, or amended on or before
August 31, 2015.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
NOTE: Refer to SB20 (84th Leg.)
for retention period context.

series_description:
Invalid bids that do not meet agency
submission requirements and are not
included in bid evaluation process (e.g.
withdrawn, missed submission
deadline, incomplete submission, etc.)
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
predecessors or maintained internally
Plans for evacuation of agency facilities
in cases of emergency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
Records relating to the issuance of
keys, identification cards, building
passes, passwords, signed statements
or similar instruments of access to
agency or state facilities,
equipment or automated systems.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Logs, registers, or similar records
documenting visitors to limited access
or restricted areas of agency facilities.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Plans developed (per Texas Labor
Code, 412.054) to mitigate the damage
of potential events that could endanger
an agency’s ability to function. Includes
disaster preparedness, response, and
recovery plans.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
